_comment: MIT General Institute Requirements (GIRs). These are the same for every undergraduate regardless of major. Source: catalog.mit.edu/mit/undergraduate-education/general-institute-requirements/

science_core._comment: 6 subjects total: Calculus I, Calculus II, Physics I, Physics II, Chemistry, Biology

calculus_i.description: Single-variable calculus. Can be fulfilled by AP BC score of 5.

physics_i.description: Classical mechanics. Can be fulfilled by AP Physics C Mechanics score of 5.

physics_ii.description: Electricity and magnetism. Requires Physics I as prerequisite.

chemistry.description: Introductory chemistry. 3.091 emphasizes solid-state; 5.111/5.112 emphasize general principles.

biology.description: Introductory biology. Multiple versions cover same core material with different themes.

rest._comment: Restricted Electives in Science and Technology — 2 required. One may be in major dept, other must be outside. Subjects are flagged 'REST' in the catalog.
rest.description: Two REST subjects required. At most one may be in your major department. REST subjects are flagged in the course catalog. Common examples: 6.1200, 6.1910, 6.3000, 6.3700, 6.2000, 8.03, 18.03, etc.
rest.rule_notes: One REST may be in the student's major department; the other must be from a different department.

lab._comment: Institute Laboratory Requirement — 1 subject (12 units) or 2 subjects (6 units each). Flagged 'Institute Lab' in catalog.
lab.description: One 12-unit Institute Lab subject, or two 6-unit Partial Lab subjects. Subjects are flagged in the catalog. Common examples: 6.1010, 6.2040, 6.2050, 6.2060, 6.3800.
lab.rule_notes: Can be fulfilled by one 12-unit lab or two 6-unit partial labs.

hass._comment: Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences — 8 subjects total with distribution and concentration requirements.

distribution.description: Must include at least one subject from each of three categories: HASS-A (Arts), HASS-H (Humanities), HASS-S (Social Sciences).

hass_a.description: At least one HASS subject in the Arts.

hass_h.description: At least one HASS subject in the Humanities.

hass_s.description: At least one HASS subject in the Social Sciences.

concentration.description: 3-4 subjects forming a coherent group in one area of HASS. At least 2 must be from the same department. The concentration must be approved.
concentration.rule_notes: At least 2 of the concentration subjects must be from the same department. One subject in the concentration may be taken on a P/D/F basis.

electives.description: Remaining HASS subjects (after distribution and concentration) are HASS electives. Any HASS-flagged subject counts.
electives.rule_notes: Up to 2 of the 8 total HASS subjects may be taken on a P/D/F basis.

communication._comment: Communication Requirement — 4 CI subjects total, paced across 4 years.

ci_h.description: Two Communication-Intensive subjects in HASS (CI-H or CI-HW). These also count toward the HASS requirement. Pacing: one should be taken freshman year, one sophomore year.
ci_h.rule_notes: CI-H subjects are offered by HASS departments. CI-HW subjects additionally fulfill a writing requirement. Both count as CI-H for this requirement.

ci_m.description: Two Communication-Intensive subjects in the Major (CI-M). These are specific to each major — see major requirements for which courses qualify.
ci_m.rule_notes: CI-M subjects are designated by each department. A list of CI-M subjects for each major is available on the department's website.

communication.pacing: One CI subject per year is recommended. CI-H subjects are typically taken in freshman and sophomore year; CI-M subjects in junior and senior year.

physical_education._comment: PE requirement — swim test + 4 PE courses (8 points). Must complete by end of sophomore year.
physical_education.description: Pass the swim test and earn 8 PE points (4 PE courses at 2 points each) by the end of sophomore year.

double_counting_notes: Between 1 and 6 subjects in a student's major may also satisfy GIRs. The units for any subject that counts as one of the 17 GIR subjects cannot also be counted as units required beyond the GIRs.
